articles.body: Item 1 of 2 People walk past an anti-U.S. mural on a street in Tehran, Iran, June 1, 2026. Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS [1/2] People walk past an anti-U.S. mural on a street in Tehran, Iran, June 1, 2026. Majid Asgaripour/WANA (West Asia News Agency) via REUTERS Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Summary Companies Iran taking 'stern' approach in negotiations, report says Trump says talks for a deal are continuing Iran pushing for a limited interim agreement, sources say Israel continues strikes on southern Lebanon DUBAI, June 2 (Reuters) - Iran is reviewing a proposed agreement with the U.S. to halt their war, Iranian media ​reported on Tuesday, after U.S. President Donald Trump said talks to reach a deal were continuing. More than three months after the U.S. and Israel launched ‌strikes against Iran, the conflict has hardened into a stalemate while efforts to negotiate an interim deal have proved inconclusive, leaving the Strait of Hormuz largely shut. Sign up here. Iran has not yet responded to a proposed final text of the temporary deal, and was taking a "stern" approach given what it sees as a history of U.S. non-compliance and longstanding mistrust, Mehr News Agency cited a source as saying. Trump said on Monday that negotiations with Iran were ​continuing and there would be a deal over the next week to extend a ceasefire agreed in early April and reopen the Strait of Hormuz. Since mid-March, Trump has ​repeatedly said he is close to signing a peace agreement, though any such deal would postpone thorny issues including the future of ⁠Iran's nuclear programme . A ceasefire has largely held since early April, but Iran and the U.S. have exchanged strikes several times over the past week. Oil prices fell more than 1% on Tuesday, ​paring the previous day's sharp gains. A senior International Energy Agency official warned that global oil inventories could hit historically low levels. ISRAEL KEEPS UP STRIKES IN LEBANON The war that began on February ​28 has killed thousands of people, mainly in Iran and Lebanon. It has caused global economic pain by pushing up energy prices since Iran effectively closed the Strait of Hormuz, which previously carried about a fifth of global supplies of oil and liquefied natural gas. It also triggered the latest round of conflict between Israel and Lebanese militant group Hezbollah, with Israel pursuing its deepest incursion into Lebanon in 25 years. On Tuesday, ​Israel kept up strikes on a string of towns in southern Lebanon, Lebanese security sources said, a day after U.S. mediation appeared to have averted an immediate escalation of that war. A ​partial ceasefire announced by Lebanon on Monday would entail Israel refraining from strikes on Beirut and Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of the Lebanese capital, while the Iran-aligned group would halt its attacks on Israel. But the ‌announcement failed ⁠to reassure many Lebanese, 1.2 million of whom have been displaced, and the din of an Israeli drone over Beirut kept residents on edge on Tuesday. "Every time we return to our homes, there is a warning for us to be displaced again," said Faten Al Chehime, who fled to a displacement camp from her home in Beirut's southern suburbs on Monday, only two weeks after returning there. Lebanon said it would seek to expand the ceasefire in talks with Israel in Washington on Wednesday. Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu is facing criticism domestically over any agreement ​to hold back from further attacks on ​Beirut, ahead of an election later this ⁠year that he is projected to lose. IRAN PUSHES FOR LIMITED DEAL In the wider war, Iran is pushing for a limited interim agreement as it tries to ease mounting economic pressure while avoiding major concessions on its nuclear programme, according to Iranian sources. As part of any deal, Tehran ​is seeking an end to hostilities across all fronts including Lebanon, access to billions of dollars in oil revenues, waivers on ​crude exports, a lifting ⁠of a U.S. blockade on its ports, and continued leverage over the Strait of Hormuz. Trump is under pressure to reopen the strait and curb U.S. fuel prices while not making concessions to Iran. Iran's elite Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps said on Tuesday that 24 vessels had transited the strait in the past 24 hours, after obtaining permission from the Guard's navy. Iran threatened on Monday to expand its blockade ⁠to the Bab ​El Mandeb Strait, another chokepoint at the mouth of the Red Sea, if Israel resumed strikes on ​Beirut. Highlighting the risk at sea, the world's largest shipping group MSC said on Tuesday that one of its vessels was struck by two projectiles while in Iraq's Umm Qasr port the previous day. Iran's Revolutionary Guards claimed responsibility for the ​attack, saying it was carried out in retaliation for a U.S. attack on an Iranian vessel in the Gulf of Oman. Reporting by Reuters bureaus; Writing by Aidan Lewis Editing by Gareth Jones Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Item 1 of 3 People take shelter inside a metro station during a Russian air strike, amid Russia's attack on Ukraine, in Kyiv, Ukraine, June 2, 2026. REUTERS/Alina Smutko [1/3] People take shelter inside a metro station during a Russian air strike, amid Russia's attack on Ukraine, in Kyiv, Ukraine, June 2, 2026. REUTERS/Alina Smutko Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab KYIV, June 2 (Reuters) - Kyiv was under heavy Russian attack early on Tuesday, ​with a large pillar of smoke seen rising ‌from the Ukrainian capital and authorities urging residents to seek shelter, according to witnesses and officials. Kyiv Mayor Vitali Klitschko said a fire ​had broken out in the Podil district on the ​grounds of a non-residential property and a nine-storey ⁠apartment building was on fire after debris apparently struck the ​roof. Sign up here. "In the Obolon district, cars are burning after being struck ​by falling missile debris. There are also fires at two locations in open areas, including one near a kindergarten," Klitschko said on Telegram. Ukrainian ​President Volodymyr Zelenskiy on Monday reiterated warnings of a possible ​massive Russian strike and urged residents to pay special attention to air ‌raid ⁠alerts. "Intelligence warnings regarding Russian strikes remain in effect. A massive strike is possible, they have prepared one," Zelenskiy said in his nightly video address. "Our defenders are ready 24/7 to ​the fullest extent ​possible with ⁠the supplies currently available." Russia last week warned that it intended to launch "systematic strikes" on targets ​in Kyiv linked to the Ukrainian military ​as well ⁠as decision-making centres, and urged foreigners to leave. It said the action was in response to a drone strike last week ⁠on ​a dormitory in Ukraine's Russian-held Luhansk ​region which killed 21 people. Ukraine denied carrying out the attack. Reporting Reuters, Writing ​by Jekaterīna Golubkova and Lincoln Feast; Editing by Himani Sarkar Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: World X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Ousmane Sonko speaks after he was appointed prime minister by Senegal's newly-elected President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, in Dakar, Senegal April 2, 2024. REUTERS/Abdou Karim Ndoye/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab DAKAR, June 1 (Reuters) - Senegal's Pastef political party will not participate in the country's new government, recently ​ousted Prime Minister Ousmane Sonko, the party president, said ‌on Monday, raising the prospect of further political turmoil amid a daunting debt crisis. Sonko said in a post on X that he ​met on Monday with President Bassirou Diomaye Faye, his ​ally-turned-rival, and that "points of disagreement" emerged about the role ⁠of Pastef, which holds a large parliamentary majority, within ​the executive branch. Sign up here. Therefore Pastef "will not participate in the next government ​and will not be represented by any ministers," Sonko said. "We wish the new team every success." Faye sacked Sonko and dissolved the government on May 22, ​then appointed seasoned economist Ahmadou Al Aminou Lo as ​a replacement. Lawmakers rebelled against Faye's decision last week by reinstating Sonko as a ‌member ⁠of parliament and overwhelmingly backing him as speaker with the support of 132 lawmakers in the 165-member assembly. The upheaval, following months of mounting tensions between Faye and Sonko, comes as ​Senegal tries to ​navigate economic ⁠woes stemming from the discovery in 2024 of misreported debt by the previous government. The International Monetary ​Fund froze its $1.8 billion lending program with ​Senegal following ⁠the discovery of the debt, which pushed the country's end-2024 debt level to 132% of its economic output. Senegal expects to ⁠resume talks ​with the IMF next week and ​hopes to reach agreement on key points by June 30, the finance minister ​said last month. Reporting by Diadie Ba; Writing by Robbie Corey-Boulet Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Africa X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

"I had ​a very productive call ​with Prime Minister Bibi Netanyahu, of Israel, and there will be ​no Troops going ​to Beirut, and any Troops that ‌are ⁠on their way, have already been turned back,” Trump said in a ​post on ​Truth ⁠Social. Sign up here. "Likewise, through highly placed Representatives, I ​had a very good ​call ⁠with Hezbollah, and they agreed that all shooting ⁠will ​stop." Reporting by ​David Ljunggren and Bhargav Acharya in ​Toronto; Editing by Doina Chiacu Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: A former Syrian intelligence service officer goes on trial in Austria charged with offences including torture and sexual assault over the abuse of opponents of then-leader Bashar al-Assad in... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more Summary Rare case of European country claiming jurisdiction in Syria Prosecution, defence give starkly contrasting accounts Defendant denies seeing torture or 'magic carpet' device 'I am innocent', he and co-defendant tell court VIENNA, June 1 (Reuters) - A former Syrian intelligence chief in the ​city of Raqqa went on trial in Austria on charges of torture and sexual abuse on ‌Monday over the mistreatment of opponents of then-leader Bashar al-Assad more than a decade ago. It is a relatively rare case of a European country claiming jurisdiction for alleged crimes committed by agents of Assad's government. The trial, due to last a month, will include ​alleged victims' testimony. Sign up here. The primary defendant, identified only as Khaled al-H under Austrian privacy rules, was head of Syria's ​General Intelligence Directorate in Raqqa from the uprising against Assad in 2011 until the Free ⁠Syrian Army took over the city in 2013, which he says he helped facilitate before fleeing the next ​day. The second defendant was a senior police official in Raqqa nicknamed "the Angel of Death," according to the prosecution. Both are charged ​with causing serious bodily harm, aggravated coercion and sexual assault. Only Khaled al-H is accused of torture. They face up to 10 years in prison if convicted. Both pleaded not guilty, and denied any involvement in torture or similar activities. "Impossible. That would not be in ​my interest. It is also not how I was raised," Khaled al-H, who is a member of Syria's Druze ​minority, said when the presiding judge asked if he had used violence against anyone in his custody. DEFENDANT DENIES COMMITTING OR WITNESSING TORTURE Both ‌sides ⁠gave vastly different accounts of conditions inside the directorate's building in Raqqa. Khaled al-H said no prisoners stayed overnight. The prosecution described cells packed with 30 or 40 people and systematic abuse, with guards beating prisoners with green garden hoses to leave fewer marks and hosing them down with cold water both to reduce signs of abuse and to make ​the next day's beatings hurt ​more. When shown drawings of ⁠different types of abuse, like beating the soles of a prisoner's feet, Khaled al-H denied ever seeing them or that they happened on his watch. He also denied ever having ​seen a torture device known as the "magic carpet" - wooden planks in the form of ​a cross with ⁠a hinge in the middle, positioned near the prisoner's waist. ASYLUM CLAIM AND INTELLIGENCE LINKS Khaled al-H came to Austria in 2015 and claimed asylum while another asylum claim in France was still pending. Despite media reports that he had been brought to Austria ⁠by a ​domestic intelligence service at the request of Israeli spy agency Mossad ​as part of "Operation White Milk," he was circumspect when asked if an intelligence service helped him come to Austria. "I don't know. I have relatives ​who helped me. How they did that, I don't know," he said. Reporting by Francois Murphy in Vienna; Editing by Matthew Lewis Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: World Human Rights X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Head of the Office of the President of Ukraine Kyrylo Budanov attends a session during the 56th annual World Economic Forum (WEF) meeting in Davos, Switzerland, January 20, 2026. REUTERS/Denis... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more KYIV, June 1 (Reuters) - Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskiy's chief of staff, Kyrylo Budanov, said on Monday he believed ​agreeing a deal to end the war against ‌Russia by winter was a "realistic" outcome. Zelenskiy said in an interview aired on Sunday that he wanted to press on ​with talks on securing peace with Russia, which ​have stalled in recent months, before the onset ⁠of winter to take account of Kyiv's improved ​strategic position. Sign up here. Talks brokered by the United States on ​moving toward a peace accord have ground to a halt as Washington has focused on the conflict in Iran. Budanov said ​he expected a U.S. delegation to visit Moscow ​and Kyiv in the near future, without giving details. "This is the ‌president's ⁠instruction: to try to end this war as soon as possible ... preferably before winter," he told reporters at a press conference. "In my opinion, this is ​absolutely correct, ​timely, and realistic." Zelenskiy ⁠and other Ukrainian officials have said the advance of Russian forces has ​slowed on the ground while Ukraine has ​intensified ⁠a campaign of long-range strikes inside Russia, targeting mainly the oil industry. A senior Ukrainian commander said last week ⁠that ​Ukraine had a six-month window in ​which to seize the battlefield initiative and strengthen its hand for ​peace talks. Reporting by Max Hunder; Editing by Kevin Liffey Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Europe X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: U.S. President Donald Trump speaks during a cabinet meeting in the Cabinet Room at the White House, in Washington, D.C., U.S., May 27, 2026. REUTERS/Evan Vucci/File Photo Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab June 1 (Reuters) - U.S. President Donald Trump said on Monday that talks with Iran ​were ongoing, despite a report that Tehran had ‌suspended indirect negotiations with the United States to end hostilities. "Talks are continuing, at a rapid pace, with the Islamic Republic of Iran," Trump ​said in a Truth Social post. Sign up here. Iranian state news agency ​Tasnim reported earlier that Tehran was halting indirect negotiations with ⁠the U.S. after Israel ordered troops to push deeper into ​Lebanon. In telephone interviews with news outlets after that report, Trump ​said he had not been told that Iran was suspending talks with Washington. "They haven't informed us of that," Trump said in an interview with NBC News. Trump said that ​silence between the two sides would be fine and he ​was willing to wait. "I think we've been talking too much if you ‌want ⁠to know the truth. I think going silent would be very good, and that could be for a long time," he told NBC. A suspension in negotiations would not mean the U.S. ​would start bombing ​Iran, Trump ⁠told the network, adding that the U.S. blockade of Iranian ports would remain in place. In ​a separate interview with CNBC, Trump had said ​he did ⁠not mind if the talks were over. "I don`t care if they`re over, honestly ... I couldn`t care less," CNBC quoted him as ⁠saying. Shortly ​after those media interviews were reported, ​Trump issued his social media post saying talks with Iran were continuing. Reporting by ​Bhargav Acharya, Ryan Jones and Ismail Shakil; Editing by Doina Chiacu Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Item 1 of 5 A demonstrator gestures as they erect a barricade during a protest against a U.S.-backed Ebola quarantine plan on the establishment of a 50-bed facility at a Kenyan air force base that was intended to host Americans exposed to Ebola, in Nanyuki town, in Laikipia County, Kenya June 1, 2026. REUTERS/John Muchucha [1/5] A demonstrator gestures as they erect a barricade during a protest against a U.S.-backed Ebola quarantine plan on the establishment of a 50-bed facility at a Kenyan air force base that was intended... Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Read more Summary Kenyan court suspends US quarantine plan after lawsuit over public health risks Kenyan officials say the facility aims to strengthen emergency response Protesters voice infection fears, demand permanent closure of planned facility by June 9 NAIROBI, June 1 (Reuters) - Hundreds of people took ​to the streets in the central Kenyan town of Nanyuki on Monday to protest moves by the ‌United States to set up an Ebola quarantine facility at a military base there, residents told Reuters, days after the High Court ordered the government to suspend the plan temporarily. The court ordered the temporary suspension on Friday after a lawsuit was brought arguing that the site ​could endanger public health. Sign up here. Senior U.S. officials said the 50-bed unit at an air force base in Laikipia county ​would serve Americans who have been exposed to the virus but are still asymptomatic. Kenya's ⁠government has also confirmed plans to set up the facility, with Health Minister Aden Duale saying in a statement on ​Saturday that it was part of a wider push to strengthen emergency response systems. The U.S. officials said the site was ​expected to have become operational last Friday. A number of military aircraft flew in and out of Nanyuki late last week and over the weekend, in what diplomats and experts said appeared to be part of ongoing U.S. preparations for the quarantine unit despite the court ​order. A Reuters witness on Saturday said police and military had increased their presence on roads leading to the air ​base. Footage obtained by Reuters on Monday showed a crowd of about 100 people standing about 4 km from the site of the planned ‌facility, ⁠blowing whistles and some riding atop a pickup. Smoke could be seen rising from something burning on the road. Local residents put the number of protesters in the hundreds. NTV Kenya and Citizen Kenya television channels showed footage of people standing by a wall outside the air base, with a tank stationed there and a handful of soldiers on guard. Patrick Wahome, ​one of the organisers of the ​protest, told Reuters that ⁠they wanted the health facility to be shut down for good by Tuesday, June 9. "Nanyuki is a very small town. The military personnel who serve the base... live with us. ​Our kids go to the same schools and that means if anyone is infected, ​we are all ⁠infected," he said. "We are picketing for our lives," he added. Cafe owner Patrick Maina said he was forced to shutter his business and described the situation as "very bad." "We haven`t opened since morning and it`s likely to be worse tomorrow,” he told Reuters. A U.S. ⁠military C-130 ​transport plane flew into Nanyuki as recently as Friday afternoon, according to ​the flight-tracking service Flightradar24. Two Nanyuki residents also reported seeing military aircraft flying towards the base over the weekend, though Reuters was unable to confirm if ​they were U.S. aircraft. Reporting by David Lewis, Noor Ali, George Obulutsa, Aaron McNicholas and Edwin Okoth; Editing by Ammu Kannampilly Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Healthcare & Pharmaceuticals X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Summary Cockroach Janta Party has 22 million followers on Instagram CJP plans protest demanding education minister step down Minister faces anger after exam paper leaks CJP an expression of online dissent against Modi's 12-year rule NEW DELHI, June 1 (Reuters) - The founder of ​an online Indian youth group that has amassed millions of followers in days said on Monday he plans ‌to take his movement on to the streets with a protest against the education minister and in a show of dissent against Prime Minister Narendra Modi. The Cockroach Janta Party has tapped into the concerns of the under-30s, who are estimated to make up more than half of India`s ​1.42 billion population, over politics, unemployment and inflation. Founder Abhijeet Dipke, who is based in the U.S., has ​focused in particular on recent leaks of exam papers and errors in marking final-year school exams ⁠that threaten to derail the careers of millions of students. Sign up here. "I will be returning to India to demand the resignation of ​the education minister," Dipke said on his personal account on X, urging young people to join a peaceful protest in ​Delhi and "exercise our constitutional right to seek accountability from the government." He said nearly 800,000 students have signed a petition seeking Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan`s resignation. The minister and a government spokesperson did not immediately respond to requests for comment. The CJP's rapid rise marks one of the largest online expressions ​of dissent against Modi`s 12-year rule , and analysts say it could dent his image despite recent state election victories, as ​frustration grows over rising fuel prices and gas shortages linked to the Iran war . The party's name is a reference to comments by India`s top ‌judge, ⁠Chief Justice Surya Kant of the Supreme Court, that compared some unemployed youth to cockroaches. Kant later said he did not mean to criticise young people but was referring to those with "fake and bogus degrees". The party has more than 22 million followers on Instagram. Item 1 of 2 The Cockroach Janta Party logo is seen in this illustration taken May 28, 2026. REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration [1/2] The Cockroach Janta Party logo is seen in this illustration taken May 28, 2026. REUTERS/Dado Ruvic/Illustration Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab DIPKE SAYS HIS FAMILY IS WORRIED HE COULD BE ARRESTED Dipke, who has lived in the U.S. for the past ​two years, said his family ​and friends were worried ⁠he could be arrested on his return. "How long can we live in fear?" he said. The government has blocked the CJP's X account and Dipke told Reuters he had to regain control ​of the party's Instagram page from unknown hackers. Senior cabinet minister Kiren Rijiju has accused the ​CJP of seeking ⁠social media followers from arch-enemy Pakistan and the "anti-India gang", while Modi has yet to comment. Rattled by anger following the cancellation of an entrance test for undergraduate medical courses last month due to a leak, the government is planning to deploy the Indian Air ⁠Force to ​transport question papers for a retest this month, instead of using the ​postal department. CJP has also focused on a youth job crisis. Government data shows that for those aged between 15 and 29, unemployment was 9.9% last year, more ​than triple the overall rate of 3.1%. Reporting by Krishna N. Das and Aftab Ahmed in New Delhi; Editing by Alexandra Hudson Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: India X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: Summary Companies Netanyahu orders Israeli military to strike south Beirut suburbs in response to Hezbollah attacks Iranian official says Israeli actions in Lebanon cause delay in diplomatic process France calls U.N. Security Council meeting on Monday BEIRUT/JERUSALEM, June 1 (Reuters) - Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ordered attacks on the Hezbollah-controlled southern suburbs of Beirut on Monday, signalling ​further escalation of a war that has complicated mediation towards resolving the U.S.-Iran conflict. Iran's foreign ministry spokesperson, Esmaeil Baghaei, said on Monday that ‌Israeli attacks in Lebanon were among factors causing a delay to the diplomatic process to end the U.S.-Iran war, reiterating that a Lebanon ceasefire was an integral part of any deal. Sign up here. Netanyahu and Defence Minister Israel Katz ordered the Israeli military to attack "terrorist targets" in the southern suburbs of Beirut, known as Dahiyeh, following Hezbollah's "repeated violations" of a ceasefire and "attacks against our cities and citizens", ​a statement from Netanyahu's office said. Having pounded Dahiyeh in the early weeks of the war, Israel has carried out only two strikes on the ​area since U.S. President Donald Trump announced a Lebanon ceasefire on April 16, even as hostilities have raged in southern ⁠Lebanon. ISRAEL CAPTURES 900-YEAR-OLD CASTLE The order follows an intensification of hostilities in the south over the weekend, with Israeli troops capturing the 900-year-old Beaufort Castle and Netanyahu ordering the ​military to expand ground operations. Lebanese authorities say more than 3,370 people have been killed in the country as a result of Israeli attacks since March 2, when Hezbollah ​opened fire at Israel in support of Iran as it came under U.S.-Israeli attack. Israel says 24 of its soldiers and four civilians have been killed over the same period. Israel has carved out a self-declared security zone in southern Lebanon where it has been razing villages, saying it aims to shield northern Israel from Hezbollah militants embedded in civilian areas. The Lebanon war has ​been the deadliest spillover of the U.S.-Israeli conflict with Iran, and has forced more than 1 million people to flee their homes, according to Lebanese authorities. Netanyahu on ​Sunday ordered the Israeli military to expand "its ground manoeuvre in Lebanon", aiming to "deepen and expand our grip on the places that were under Hezbollah's control". An Israeli tank maneuvers in southern Lebanon, as seen from northern Israel, May 31, 2026. REUTERS/Amir Cohen Purchase Licensing Rights , opens new tab Accusing Israel of ceasefire violations and ‌declaring the ⁠right to resist Israeli occupation, Hezbollah said it carried out 21 operations on Sunday, including firing a rocket salvo at what it described as Israeli military infrastructure in the Israeli city of Nahariya. FRANCE CALLS SECURITY COUNCIL MEETING Citing the escalating violence in Lebanon, France called for an emergency meeting of the United Nations Security Council on Monday. The United States has hosted a series of rare meetings between representatives of the governments of Israel and Lebanon since the hostilities erupted, with Beirut attending despite strong ​objections from Hezbollah. But a Lebanese source familiar ​with the diplomacy between Beirut and ⁠Washington said Netanyahu's announcement on Monday reflected the deterioration of the U.S.-led diplomatic track in recent days. A U.S. official said on Sunday that Secretary of State Marco Rubio spoke with both Lebanese President Joseph Aoun and Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu ​on the diplomatic negotiations between Israel and Lebanon and that he had proposed a plan to allow for "gradual de-escalation". The ​U.S. has proposed that, ⁠as a first step, Hezbollah would stop all attacks on Israel and in return Israel would refrain from escalation in Beirut, the official said. "This would create space for gradual de-escalation and an effective cessation of hostilities," according to the official. The official added that Aoun tried to advance the proposal and secure an agreement. However, Lebanese Parliament Speaker Nabih ⁠Berri, who ​claimed to "guarantee" Hezbollah's commitment to a ceasefire, placed the burden on Israel to stop "shooting first". Berri, a ​close ally of Hezbollah, in a comment reported by Lebanese media on Sunday, said he would guarantee "full and immediate commitment to a ceasefire by the resistance", referring to Hezbollah. "But the question is, who will ​compel Israel to stop its aggression?" he said. Reporting by Rami Ayyub in Jerusalem; Writing by Menna Alaa El-Din and Tom Perry; Editing by Sharon Singleton and Alex Richardson Our Standards: The Thomson Reuters Trust Principles. , opens new tab Suggested Topics: Middle East X Facebook Linkedin Email Link Purchase Licensing Rights

articles.body: It is the moving story of a Russian father and daughter who opposed Vladimir Putin’s war in Ukraine. It all began in March 2022, when 12-year-old Maria drew an anti-war picture at school in response to the invasion launched a month earlier. She was immediately reported by those around her, and the FSB soon visited both her school and her home. Her father was eventually arrested for supporting his daughter and speaking out against the war. He was convicted of discrediting the Russian army. Following his release from a penal colony, he and Maria left Russia and are now refugees in Strasbourg. A report by Elena Volochine, English adaptation by Lauren Bain.

articles.body: <p>El sumario del <i>caso Leire Díez</i> que se investiga en la Audiencia Nacional muestra distintas reuniones que la exmilitante del PSOE mantuvo con guardias civiles, fiscales, políticos, investigados en causas de hidrocarburos e, incluso, el comisario José Manuel Villarejo con la intención, presuntamente, de torpedear los casos de corrupción que afectaban al Partido Socialista. El audio de uno de los encuentros que se celebró el 10 de diciembre de 2024 evidencia cómo Díez hablaba de la directora de la Guardia Civil, Mercedes González, como alguien de su “confianza”. En la cita estuvo ella, un exagente de la Unidad Central Operativa (UCO) del instituto armado, el capitán Juan Yepes, el abogado Jacobo Teijelo y el empresario Javier Pérez Dolset, todos ellos imputados en esta investigación. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/los-audios-del-caso-leire-diez-mi-siguiente-conversacion-va-a-ser-con-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil-es-de-mi-confianza.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>El ministro de Interior, Fernando Grande-Marlaska, ha defendido con rotundidad a la directora general de la Guardia Civil, Mercedes González. “A mí me consta su actuación ejemplar y plena honestidad”, ha señalado Grande-Marlaska a su llegada a Luxemburgo para una reunión con los ministros de Interior de la UE. “No he conocido ninguna actuación dirigida a menoscabar el trabajo de los cuerpos y fuerzas de seguridad del Estado, y en este caso de la Guardia Civil. No lo hubiera tolerado”, ha apuntado el ministro el día después de que se supiera que en el sumario del <i>caso Leire Díez</i> aparece que <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/el-caso-leire-diez-golpea-a-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil-por-las-conversaciones-y-contactos-con-la-fontanera.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/el-caso-leire-diez-golpea-a-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil-por-las-conversaciones-y-contactos-con-la-fontanera.html">González se reunió en, al menos, tres ocasiones con la investigada y que mantuvo alguna llamada telefónica</a>. Del lado de la oposición, el Partido Popular exige la dimisión tanto de Grande-Marlaska como de González por las últimas revelaciones judiciales y vuelve a pedir elecciones anticipadas. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/marlaska-me-consta-la-actuacion-ejemplar-y-plena-honestidad-de-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p><a href="https://elpais.com/noticias/david-sanchez-perez-castejon/" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/noticias/david-sanchez-perez-castejon/">David Sánchez, hermano del presidente del Gobierno</a>, ha negado este jueves ante el tribunal que le juzga por las supuestas irregularidades en la adjudicación a él en 2017 y a un amigo suyo en 2023 de sendas plazas en la Diputación de Badajoz que él tuviera capacidad para “influir en nada”. Sánchez, cuya declaración solo ha durado 10 minutos al contestar únicamente a las preguntas de su abogado defensor, ha puesto en valor el trabajo que desempeñaba para hacer frente a las acusaciones de absentismo laboral que esgrimen las acusaciones populares como un indicio contra él en las acusaciones de tráfico de influencias y prevaricación por las que piden tres años de cárcel. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/david-sanchez-no-tengo-capacidad-de-influir-en-nada.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/TRLPBJ7GWBHQJD5IXBWBIN55IE.JPG?auth=f58228c75812899fbf25b19e09b1ae10310c3793f7b87d43b909a4379e8cda3f" width="3000" height="2001" alt="Carteles de Adelante Andalucía y de la coalición Por Andalucía, en Cádiz durante la pasada campaña electoral autonómica."></img><p>Los recientes resultados en cuatro elecciones regionales en España sugieren que las izquierdas más pegadas al territorio y con identidad regionalista o nacionalista se desempeñan mejor que la izquierda de proyección nacional. El debate surge justo cuando la izquierda española trata de encontrar una fórmula estratégica para maximizar sus votos en toda España de cara a unas generales. ¿La vía del éxito está en los proyectos territoriales?</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/opinion/2026-06-03/el-debate-la-izquierda-tiene-que-asumir-identidades-territoriales-para-crecer-electoralmente.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>No es Wimbledon, ni Roland Garros, ni el Mundial. Un partido de un deporte muy distinto se juega ante nuestros ojos y nos vuelve locos. Y aquí no están Federer o Djokovic sudando la camiseta frente a Nadal o Alcaraz, como en los mejores tiempos, seguidos hasta el último aliento por los españoles deseosos de llevarse una alegría, sino algo muy diferente. A un lado: <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-29/periodista-de-investigacion-o-fontanera-a-sueldo-las-contradicciones-de-leire-diez.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-29/periodista-de-investigacion-o-fontanera-a-sueldo-las-contradicciones-de-leire-diez.html">las causas contra el PSOE,</a> que el contrario aplaude y comenta a rabiar. Y al otro: <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-29/la-figura-de-cospedal-vuelve-al-juicio-de-kitchen-dice-que-hablaba-con-villarejo-por-el-bien-del-estado.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-29/la-figura-de-cospedal-vuelve-al-juicio-de-kitchen-dice-que-hablaba-con-villarejo-por-el-bien-del-estado.html">las causas contra el PP,</a> que no tienen tanta celebración porque ya están amortizadas. <a href="https://elpais.com/politica/2018/06/02/actualidad/1527959991_700482.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/politica/2018/06/02/actualidad/1527959991_700482.html">El PP ya cayó del podio.</a></p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/opinion/2026-05-30/conspiracion-merecemos-una-explicacion-algo-mejor.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>La democracia necesita que todos y cada uno de los actores ejerzan diligentemente el rol que tienen encomendado. Según la tradición, Godiva pidió a su marido, señor de Coventry, que aliviara la carga fiscal de sus vecinos. Él, intuyendo que la humillación sería más eficaz que la compasión, le impuso una condición cruel: atravesar la ciudad desnuda montada a caballo. Ella aceptó, pero pidió a los vecinos que se metieran en sus casas y cerraran puertas y ventanas.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/opinion/2026-05-29/la-decencia-de-cerrar-las-ventanas.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>El verdadero misterio político de Zapatero no está enterrado en <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-26/zapatero-se-enfrenta-a-una-investigacion-de-anos-con-ramificaciones-en-venezuela-y-china.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-26/zapatero-se-enfrenta-a-una-investigacion-de-anos-con-ramificaciones-en-venezuela-y-china.html">los folios de una instrucción judicial.</a> Lo que de allí salga, limpio o sucio, no aclarará <a href="https://elpais.com/opinion/2026-05-20/cuando-lo-politico-es-demasiado-personal-y-viceversa.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/opinion/2026-05-20/cuando-lo-politico-es-demasiado-personal-y-viceversa.html">el enigma de su figura,</a> cuyo brillo tanto deslumbra a los ojos más destacados de la izquierda, así en el PSOE como en sus galerías aledañas. Sobre todo, en sus galerías aledañas, donde insisten mucho en su condición de referente ideológico, de faro histórico que guía entre la niebla a las generaciones presentes. La santidad progresista del expresidente se muestra evidente en sí misma incluso desde la derecha, que pasó de llamarle <i>Bambi </i>a pintarle cuernos, rabo y tridente diabólico. Con él empezó todo, gritan.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/opinion/2026-05-27/el-mito-izquierdista-de-zapatero-y-la-memoria-selectiva.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/la-uco-atribuye-al-exlider-socialista-miguel-angel-gallardo-la-decision-politica-de-contratar-a-david-sanchez-en-la-diputacion-de-badajoz.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/la-uco-atribuye-al-exlider-socialista-miguel-angel-gallardo-la-decision-politica-de-contratar-a-david-sanchez-en-la-diputacion-de-badajoz.html">El expresidente de la Diputación de Badajoz Miguel Ángel Gallardo</a> ha dedicado la poco más de media hora que ha durado su declaración ante el tribunal que le juzga por las supuestas irregularidades en la adjudicación en 2017 a David Sánchez, hermano del presidente del Gobierno, de un puesto de alta dirección en la Diputación de Badajoz que encabezaba, a negar de manera reiterada cualquier irregularidad. Durante su intervención, Gallardo ha aprovechado para cargar tanto contra las acusaciones populares ―a las que ha calificado de “políticas”, lo que le ha costado una reprimenda del presidente del tribunal, el magistrado José Antonio Patrocinio― como contra la Unidad Central Operativa (UCO) de la Guardia Civil, cuyos informes le señalan como el presunto instigador político de las supuestas maniobras para que el familiar de Pedro Sánchez obtuviera la plaza. “Ayer yo asistí a una novela de ficción”, ha afirmado sobre la declaración el miércoles del teniente coronel Antonio Balas, máximo responsable policial de las pesquisas. El presidente del tribunal le ha recriminado también estas palabras.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/miguel-angel-gallardo-tilda-los-informes-de-la-uco-de-novela-de-ficcion.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>Agentes de la Unidad Central Operativa (UCO) de la Guardia Civil han acudido este jueves a la sede central de Tubos Reunidos en Amurrio (Álava) y a sus oficinas en Bilbao para realizar un registro por orden del juez de la Audiencia Nacional Santiago Pedraz en el marco de una pieza del <i>caso Leire</i> que mantiene bajo secreto, según han confirmado a EL PAÍS fuentes conocedoras del sumario. Además, la unidad de la Guardia Civil ha solicitado información a la Sociedad Estatal de Participaciones Industriales (SEPI) sobre la compañía.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/la-uco-registra-la-sede-de-tubos-reunidos-en-bilbao-por-una-pieza-secreta-del-caso-leire.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p><a href="https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://elpais.com/noticias/sala-de-lo-penal/&ved=2ahUKEwiCiobRve2UAxU_UqQEHQXlLIAQFnoECBsQAQ&usg=AOvVaw1b7jdbSjFUQ5DQzvyy4oFq" target="_self" rel="" title="https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://elpais.com/noticias/sala-de-lo-penal/&ved=2ahUKEwiCiobRve2UAxU_UqQEHQXlLIAQFnoECBsQAQ&usg=AOvVaw1b7jdbSjFUQ5DQzvyy4oFq">La Sala Penal del Tribunal Supremo</a> ha admitido a trámite la querella presentada por la Fiscalía contra el <a href="https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://elpais.com/noticias/senado/&ved=2ahUKEwjR05q5ve2UAxXnVaQEHdzpGAsQFnoECBwQAQ&usg=AOvVaw3MxCB6BwUCXQccc-k61z3i" target="_self" rel="" title="https://www.google.com/url?sa=t&source=web&rct=j&opi=89978449&url=https://elpais.com/noticias/senado/&ved=2ahUKEwjR05q5ve2UAxXnVaQEHdzpGAsQFnoECBwQAQ&usg=AOvVaw3MxCB6BwUCXQccc-k61z3i">senador del PSOE</a> y alcalde de Villaluenga del Rosario (Cádiz), Alfonso Carlos Moscoso, por proponer a su mujer, afiliada al partido, para ocupar una plaza como jueza de Paz en su pueblo. El Supremo abre causa también a la mujer del senador, ya que, a pesar de que no esté aforada, los hechos que se le imputan mantienen “una conexión material inescindible”, o que no se puede separar, con los que se le atribuyen al parlamentario, según señala el Alto Tribunal. Se abre causa para ambos por un presunto delito de prevaricación de funcionarios públicos.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/el-supremo-abre-una-causa-contra-un-senador-del-psoe-por-proponer-el-nombramiento-de-su-esposa-como-jueza-de-paz.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>El presidente del Gobierno, Pedro Sánchez, acompañará al papa León XIV en el encuentro que mantendrá el Pontífice con <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-22/canarias-punto-de-encuentro-entre-el-papa-y-los-migrantes-el-sabe-lo-que-vivimos.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-05-22/canarias-punto-de-encuentro-entre-el-papa-y-los-migrantes-el-sabe-lo-que-vivimos.html">migrantes, trabajadores y voluntarios que los atienden en el muelle del puerto de Arguineguín</a>. Junto a Sánchez, se desplazarán al muelle grancanario el ministro de la Presidencia y Justicia, Félix Bolaños; el ministro de Política Territorial y Memoria Democrática, Ángel Víctor Torres; y la ministra de Migraciones, Elma Saiz. El muelle de Arguineguín se convirtió en uno de los grandes símbolos de la crisis migratoria en 2020 cuando se improvisó allí un campamento en el que llegaron a hacinarse cerca de 3.000 personas en apenas 200 metros que habían llegado en patera a través de la ruta atlántica. El próximo 11 de junio, varios migrantes que emprendieron esta travesía relatarán su experiencia ante el Papa durante la que será la primera visita de un pontífice a Canarias.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/sanchez-acompanara-al-papa-leon-xiv-en-su-encuentro-con-migrantes-en-el-muelle-de-arguineguin.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>Cuatro años después del naufragio del <a href="https://elpais.com/noticias/villa-de-pitanxo/" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/noticias/villa-de-pitanxo/">pesquero gallego Villa de Pitanxo</a> en aguas de Terranova (Canadá) en el que murieron 21 tripulantes (de los cuales solo pudieron recuperarse nueve cuerpos y 12 continúan desaparecidos), la Fiscalía de la Audiencia Nacional ha presentado su escrito de acusación en el que solicita nueve años de prisión por homicidio imprudente para el capitán del pesquero, Juan Enrique Padín Costas, y los propietarios de la empresa armadora del buque, José Antonio Nores Rodríguez y José Antonio Nores Ortega. La mercantil Pesquerías Nores, con sede en Marín (Pontevedra), y la entidad aseguradora British Marine serán responsables civiles en este proceso. Los tres acusados se enfrentan a 13 años de inhabilitación para desempeñar actividades en el sector pesquero. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/la-fiscalia-pide-nueve-anos-de-prision-para-el-capitan-del-villa-de-pitanxo-y-dos-armadores-del-buque-por-la-muerte-de-21-tripulantes.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>Las conversaciones de WhatsApp de la exmilitante del PSOE, las anotaciones en su agenda y el posicionamiento de su teléfono móvil apuntan a que Leire Díez mantuvo, al menos, tres reuniones presenciales con la directora de la Guardia Civil, Mercedes González, además de alguna llamada telefónica. En los mensajes que ha analizado la Unidad Central Operativa (UCO) del instituto armado, Díez se refiere al alto cargo del Ministerio del Interior como una persona cercana (“Tengo que hablar con Mercedes. No hay confianza con Marlaska”), a pesar de que fuentes del Ejecutivo aseguran que la exmilitante, ahora investigada en la Audiencia Nacional, nunca influyó en ninguna operación o investigación interna del cuerpo. El sumario conocido este miércoles expone que se abrieron tres informaciones reservadas (investigaciones internas) contra agentes de la UCO que intervenían en pesquisas sobre la supuesta corrupción del Gobierno y que un general del cuerpo relató cómo le pidieron información detallada de todos los agentes que investigaban el entorno del presidente del Ejecutivo, Pedro Sánchez.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/el-caso-leire-diez-golpea-a-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil-por-las-conversaciones-y-contactos-con-la-fontanera.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p><img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/Y33UMDZZTNHIBBIJUNCHS7ZK24.png?auth=37e20a2245689894836e66466dd7882ff9917316eccd6fd4b034f8550105fd80" width="563" height="181" alt="Anotación de la agenda de Leire Díez"></img>

articles.body: <img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/D4OCNRBVJ5C5JEQMVMEZF6DOHE.jpg?auth=671bde900014e839cf44f7b446be8c59790278ab0b107fb018223ba77d5a4974" width="1200" height="675" alt="."></img><p>La UCO constató <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/la-uco-constata-que-leire-diez-se-reunio-tres-veces-con-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil-a-la-que-insto-a-iniciar-una-investigacion-interna.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/la-uco-constata-que-leire-diez-se-reunio-tres-veces-con-la-directora-de-la-guardia-civil-a-la-que-insto-a-iniciar-una-investigacion-interna.html">“al menos” tres reuniones</a> de la supuesta <i>fontanera</i> del PSOE Leire Díez con la directora de la Guardia Civil, Mercedes González, a la que supuestamente instó a iniciar una “investigación interna” contra la unidad, según recoge el <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/la-uco-apunta-que-leire-diez-tenia-contacto-directo-con-el-fiscal-general-del-estado-te-ha-escrito-el-fge.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/la-uco-apunta-que-leire-diez-tenia-contacto-directo-con-el-fiscal-general-del-estado-te-ha-escrito-el-fge.html">sumario del <i>caso Leire,</i></a> que señala además que Díez y González mantuvieron además conversaciones de WhatsApp con Mercedes González, que esta borró. El informe de la Unidad Central Operativa de la Guardia Civil, incorporado a la causa que instruye la Audiencia Nacional contra una supuesta trama que obró para torpedear investigaciones que afectaban a ex altos cargos <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/el-psoe-circunscribe-a-cerdan-y-diez-la-trama-para-desprestigiar-a-jueces-fiscales-e-instituciones-del-estado.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/el-psoe-circunscribe-a-cerdan-y-diez-la-trama-para-desprestigiar-a-jueces-fiscales-e-instituciones-del-estado.html">del PSOE</a> y del Gobierno, detalla cómo Díez presumía de tener “confianza” con la directora general ante otros miembros de la presunta organización investigada.</p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-04/los-chats-de-leire-diez-con-los-miembros-de-la-trama-para-influir-en-la-jefatura-de-la-guardia-civil-tengo-amistad-con-la-directora-general.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p><img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/VFA2FJU5ZVDH3MIZCOY4AWSNB4.jpg?auth=43cc4b93a242e7e28a5525b6a906daf238d3d1c034c873c4bd9ca10ca776f674" width="1850" height="1849"></img><h3>Vicente Fernández</h3>Dos cosas: 1) ¿Cómo te fue con la Benemérita? 2) No me mandaste el móvil del abogado que me dijiste. ¿Llegaste a volver a hablar con él?<img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/CQ6UWRPX4ND2XJLLHNDQSUJBQY.png?auth=730ee130bae8fcc91a9cde27ab3277db9dc471a1fb861f5fe842f6e1df37e58a" width="500" height="500"></img><h3>Leire Díez</h3>Le he llamado, pero no me ha cogido Con la benemérita bien Ya tengo las causas que tenia en papel digitalizadas Miraré el nombre que me dijiste <img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/VFA2FJU5ZVDH3MIZCOY4AWSNB4.jpg?auth=43cc4b93a242e7e28a5525b6a906daf238d3d1c034c873c4bd9ca10ca776f674" width="1850" height="1849"></img><h3>Vicente Fernández</h3>Ok<img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/CQ6UWRPX4ND2XJLLHNDQSUJBQY.png?auth=730ee130bae8fcc91a9cde27ab3277db9dc471a1fb861f5fe842f6e1df37e58a" width="500" height="500"></img><h3>Leire Díez</h3>Le he comentado un poco por encima lo de los parkings. articles.body: <p>El contenido del <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/agendas-de-cerdan-facturas-de-viajes-de-diez-y-emails-del-psoe-entre-la-documentacion-que-la-uco-se-llevo-de-ferraz.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/agendas-de-cerdan-facturas-de-viajes-de-diez-y-emails-del-psoe-entre-la-documentacion-que-la-uco-se-llevo-de-ferraz.html">nuevo sumario</a> al que se le ha levantado el secreto es más grave que los anteriores, en especial porque ya no se refiere a un expresidente del Gobierno, o a excolaboradores muy cercanos del actual, sino que apunta directamente al jefe del Ejecutivo y líder socialista, Pedro Sánchez. Lo hace, eso sí, utilizando una de esas expresiones que llevan mucha carga explosiva y poca definición de hechos concretos. Se trata de una de esas construcciones verbales en forma de remolino, para sostener que el “fin último” de las actuaciones atribuidas a la fontanera del PSOE, Leire Díez, era “proteger los intereses que afectan directa o indirectamente al Gobierno o <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/un-jefe-judicial-de-la-guardia-civil-acuso-al-exdirector-de-instar-a-acelerar-un-informe-sobre-david-sanchez-y-concluir-que-no-habia-nada.html" target="_blank" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-03/un-jefe-judicial-de-la-guardia-civil-acuso-al-exdirector-de-instar-a-acelerar-un-informe-sobre-david-sanchez-y-concluir-que-no-habia-nada.html">a su presidente</a>”. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/2026-06-05/la-tesis-del-fin-ultimo-y-las-necesarias-explicaciones.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <img src="https://imagenes.elpais.com/resizer/v2/UZYOQFTSQREYBKAHDMVPECK6CE.jpg?auth=10e0d6a2a20af9d66303084f72710093f2199919a5555fdb91b4ea6dbc5623d3" width="5715" height="4000" alt="Juan María Nin, presidente del Círculo de Empresarios, en una imagen de archivo"></img><p>El Círculo de Empresarios pide “un nuevo mandato ciudadano” y “nuevos acuerdos políticos para gobernar”, con el objetivo de abordar “las reformas estructurales que la situación de cambio mundial exige”. El centro de análisis fundado en 1977 asegura en un comunicado divulgado este viernes que “la prolongada ausencia de<a href="https://elpais.com/espana/catalunya/2026-06-03/pedro-sanchez-anuncia-que-el-gobierno-presentara-el-proyecto-de-presupuestos-para-2027.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/catalunya/2026-06-03/pedro-sanchez-anuncia-que-el-gobierno-presentara-el-proyecto-de-presupuestos-para-2027.html"> Presupuestos Generales del Estado</a>, la polarización y el deterioro del capital institucional debilitan la capacidad efectiva de gobierno”, lo que dificulta “tomar las decisiones que reclama España”. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/economia/2026-06-04/el-circulo-de-empresarios-pide-un-nuevo-mandato-ciudadano-para-acometer-las-reformas-que-reclama-espana.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: <p>El PP <a href="https://elpais.com/espana/elecciones-castilla-y-leon/2026-06-03/el-pp-y-vox-alcanzan-un-acuerdo-en-castilla-y-leon-para-investir-a-manueco-que-incluye-la-prioridad-nacional.html" target="_self" rel="" title="https://elpais.com/espana/elecciones-castilla-y-leon/2026-06-03/el-pp-y-vox-alcanzan-un-acuerdo-en-castilla-y-leon-para-investir-a-manueco-que-incluye-la-prioridad-nacional.html">ha alcanzado un acuerdo con su antiguo socio de Gobierno</a>, Vox, para reeditar la coalición en Castilla y León. Tras los pactos los populares y la ultraderecha en Extremadura y Aragón, ambas fuerzas han acordado un documento con 19 medidas que profundiza en su relación de socios y responde a buena parte de las demandas y el argumentario de la extrema derecha en materia migratoria. Inmigración y políticas fiscales están en el centro de un acuerdo en el que se hace además gran hincapié en un nuevo marco para la gestión de los incendios. </p><p><a href="https://elpais.com/espana/elecciones-castilla-y-leon/2026-06-04/pp-y-vox-ahondan-en-su-relacion-de-socios-en-castilla-y-leon-de-la-ley-de-concordia-al-requisito-del-empadronamiento-en-la-comunidad-y-en-espana.html" target="_blank">Seguir leyendo</a></p>

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida Torna el festival Cruïlla a Barcelona amb una edició, la del 2026, que celebrarà els 16 anys ! I, de nou, tindrà com a escenari el Parc del Fòrum , els dies 8, 9, 10 i 11 de juliol . Hi haurà noms destacats com Pixies , David Byrne , Suede , Two Door Cinema Club, The Black Crowes i The Hives i grups locals catalans com Mishima , Sidonie , Judeline, Rigoberta Bandini, La Ludwig Band, Alizzz i Els Pets, entre altres. En aquesta pàgina podràs anar consultant tota la informació que sabem del festival barceloní. Ara ja tens els horaris de cada concert. Preus i entrades del Cruïlla abonament de cap de setmana : 138 € abonament de quatre dies : 158 € entrades per dies : de 75 a 89 € Què vols saber sobre el festival Cruïlla 2026? Cruïlla 2026 a Barcelona: dates El festival es farà el 8, 9, 10 i 11 de juliol del 2026 al Parc del Fòrum de Barcelona . del 8 a l’11 de juliol 2026 Pixies, David Byrne o Mishima, primers confirmats al Cruïlla 2026 Manel Carrasco 1 minut de lectura Com arribar i marxar del Cruïlla 2026 Bus llançadora Aquest any hi haurà un servei de busos llançadora que tindrà un preu de 2,5 € i que es podrà abonar amb targeta o en efectiu en el moment d’agafar el bus. El punt de sortida serà davant l’hotel Princess i el punt de descàrrega, a la ronda de Sant Pere amb el passeig de Gràcia. Els busos funcionaran la nit de dijous a divendres, de 0 a 2 h, i la nit de divendres a dissabte, de 2 a 6 h. articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida Aquesta és l’agenda de concerts a Barcelona . T’agrada gaudir de la música en directe ? Vols saber quina música pots escoltar a L’Auditori de Barcelona, al Palau de la Música o al Liceu? Tots els estils i totes les sales: aquí trobaràs els concerts setmana a setmana a la nostra ciutat . I et recordem que en altres sales de la ciutat com Razzmatazz, Apolo i Paral·lel 62 trobaràs sempre música en directe. I també pot gaudir de la música en directe a El Molino, amb concerts de jazz, soul i blues. I, és clar, els festivals com Sónar , Primavera Sound o Cruïlla d’estiu i en la seva edició Cruïlla d’hivern . També el Guitar BCN , Soundit , Sons de ciutat , Tradicionàrius , Barnasants , la Telecogresca . I la festa de 150 anys d’Estrella Damm . I recorda que si vols recuperar concerts d’alguns dels festivals que es fan a la ciutat o dels que s’han fet a les festes de la Mercè de Barcelona, ho pots fer al Música moderna . Vols rebre més plans com aquest abans de ningú? Subscriu-te al butlletí ‘Què fer a Barcelona’ i rep cada divendres una selecció de propostes (moltes gratuïtes) per al cap de setmana 👉 Ho faig ara mateix! articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida (ACN) La Comissió Europea ha obert un procediment d’infracció contra Espanya per incompliment de les normes de protecció de dades de la UE en la recollida d’ informació de viatgers . Concretament, l’executiu comunitari considera que el Govern espanyol infringeix la directiva sobre protecció de dades en l’ àmbit policial —la normativa que regula el tractament de dades personals per part de les autoritats policials— perquè Espanya obliga els proveïdors d’allotjament, plataformes en línia i empreses de lloguer de vehicles “a recollir, conservar i transmetre dades personals dels viatgers en una base de dades governamental centralitzada accessible per a les autoritats policials”. Dades “excessives” de pagament i geolocalització Segons la Comissió Europea, aquesta obligació incompleix els requisits de la directiva. En aquest sentit, ha indicat “que les categories de dades personals recollides i emmagatzemades són excessives, ja que inclouen múltiples conjunts de dades, com ara dades de pagament i de geolocalització (GPS)”. A més, Brussel·les ha alertat que l’accés per part de les autoritats policials “no està limitat a finalitats específiques i explícites, com exigeix la normativa”. També et pot interessar L’OCU alerta de risc de “phishing” als clients de Naturgy afectats per la filtració de dades Helena Molist Sancho 1 minut de lectura Tres anys, un temps “desproporcionat” per retenir les dades La Comissió Europea també considera “desproporcionat” que les autoritats espanyoles retinguin totes les dades durant tres anys . Per aquest motiu, ha enviat una carta al Govern espanyol, que ara disposa de dos mesos per respondre i corregir les deficiències detectades. En cas contrari, Brussel·les podria emetre un dictamen motivat i, en última instància, podria denunciar Espanya davant del Tribunal de Justícia de la Unió Europea (TJUE).

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida (ACN/Redacció) Dues hores després de conèixer el resultat de la consulta sobre el preacord d’Educació, la consellera, Esther Niubó, ha expressat el “ compromís ” del govern per desplegar-lo, malgrat el no de la majoria de docents que han votat en la consulta . En una roda de premsa al Parlament, Niubó ha demanat responsabilitat als sindicats i ha dit que fa falta una “ reflexió profunda “. Així, ha agraït el suport del Sindicat de Secundària i ha lamentat que la USTEC s’hagi “desvinculat” del pacte. Per això, ha anunciat que mantindrà una “ ronda de contactes ” amb els sindicats signants. També et pot interessar Els docents rebutgen el preacord amb Educació i voten a favor de seguir amb la vaga Marta Casals Torres 3 minuts de lectura Els sindicats criden a la vaga Després de conèixer els resultats de la consulta, els sindicats  USTEC-STEs, CGT i Intersindical  han anunciat que mantenen la vaga que estava prevista per a aquest  divendres, 5 de juny , i també la manifestació que  arrencarà a les 12 h a l’Arc de Triomf . A més, asseguren que estudiaran, durant els propers dies,  altres vagues i mobilitzacions . També et pot interessar Quins dies hi ha vaga d’educació a Barcelona? Dates al maig i juny i calendari complet Eva Vial 3 minuts de lectura Per la seva banda, la CGT i la Intersindical han reclamat el “ cessament immediat ” de la consellera. Creuen que el conflicte “se li ha escapat de les mans”. També et pot interessar Els sindicats educatius criden a la vaga després del no al preacord i la CGT demana destituir Niubó Maria Solans 2 minuts de lectura

articles.body: Afegeix betevé com a font preferida (ACN)  La patronal europea dels aeroports Airports Council International Europe (ACI Europe) ha registrat un 0,7 % menys de passatgers aeris a l’abril arreu d’Europa en comparació amb el 2025. Aquesta és la primera caiguda, en termes interanuals, des de l’abril del 2021, quan consideren que va començar la recuperació de la pandèmia del coronavirus . La patronal ha explicat en un comunicat publicat aquest dijous que hi ha hagut una “combinació de factors”, com la guerra a l’Orient Mitjà o les vacances de Setmana Santa al març. L’ aeroport de Barcelona ha sigut l’aeròdrom que més ha crescut entre els principals aeroports, els que mouen més de 40 milions de passatgers anuals. La infraestructura catalana va transportar un 4,4 % més de viatgers a l’abril. També et pot interessar L’aeroport de Barcelona-el Prat vol consolidar-se com a punt de connexió intercontinental Helena Molist Sancho Jaume Planagumà 2 minuts de lectura Barcelona lidera el creixement de passatgers Barcelona va liderar el creixement entre els aeroports més grans del continent, amb més de 40 milions de passatgers anuals, seguit de Madrid (3,3 %) i Amsterdam Schiphol (2,7 %). París CDG es va quedar estable i Roma Fiumicino va perdre un 0,6 % dels seus viatgers en comparació amb l’abril del 2025. Tot i això, l’aeroport que més ha crescut a Europa va ser el d’ Estocolm Bromma , considerat petit per transportar menys d’un milió de viatgers, en un 699,8 %. Es tracta d’un aeroport regional principalment destinat a l’ús de privats. D’altra banda, Espanya va ser el país entre els principals europeus que més va créixer en nombre de passatgers (3,7 %). Itàlia també va registrar una crescuda (2,2 %), mentre que va haver-hi menys viatgers a Alemanya (-8,5 %), el Regne Unit (-2,1 %) i França (-0,9 %). També et pot interessar Barcelona rep el triple de turistes que fa 25 anys i es proposa “no créixer més” Redacció 3 minuts de lectura Quins països perden turisme aeri? La principal caiguda es va registrar als aeroports de fora del bloc comunitari, un 7,6 % menys de mitjana. Israel va perdre el 73,4 % dels seus passatgers; Geòrgia, el 16,3 % i l’Azerbaidjan, el 12,9%. Mentre que els aeroports de Macedònia del Nord en van comptabilitzar 30,6 % més.

articles.body: L’Espanyol engegarà una temporada amb il·lusions renovades després de mantenir-se a Primera Divisió, la categoria en què es vol quedar. La incorporació de Monchi com a director general esportiu ha revitalitzat una afició que pensa a fer-la grossa amb Manolo González, de nou, a la banqueta . Per aconseguir-ho, això sí, caldrà fer una bona pretemporada i aquí et deixem els amistosos previstos per a aquest estiu . La UE Olot, primer rival de l’Espanyol aquest estiu L’Espanyol s’enfrontarà a la UE Olot el pròxim 18 de juliol, a les 19 h, a l’Estadi Municipal d’Olot . La UE Olot, que competeix a la Segona RFEF, va finalitzar la temporada passada en la dotzena posició del grup 3. El conjunt gironí serà el primer adversari del RCD Espanyol en una fase de treball clau per consolidar conceptes i acumular minuts de competició abans de l’inici oficial de la temporada. També et pot interessar Manolo González posa nota a la temporada de l’Espanyol després de confirmar-se que hi continuarà 2 minuts de lectura Tots els amistosos de l’Espanyol de la pretemporada 2026-27 UE Olot dissabte, 18 de juliol (19 h) Estadi Municipal d’Olot Burnley FC dimecres, 29 de juliol (20.45 h) Estadi Turf Moor

articles.body: L’ Espanyol i Romane Salvador han arribat a un acord per renovar el contracte de la portera per a una temporada més, per tant, fins al 30 de juny del 2027 . D’aquesta manera, el club blanc-i-blau s’assegura la continuïtat d’una jugadora clau en els esquemes de Sara Monforte. Romane Salvador, una garantia sota pals Després de la renovació de Sara Monforte , ara ha arribat el torn de Romane Salvador . Des que va arribar a l’Espanyol, el setembre del 2023, la portera s’ha convertit en una de les peces importants de l’equip. De fet, destaca per ser una garantia sota pals : és una portera regular, segura i que té capacitat per aparèixer en els moments decisius dels partits. Aquesta temporada, la 2025-26, Romane Salvador ha jugat un total de 25 partits com a titular i ha acumulat un total de 2.250 minuts. Ha aconseguit mantenir la porteria a zero durant vuit partits i ha estat clau en la salvació de l’equip. Amb la seva continuïtat, l’Espanyol manté l’estructura a la porteria, ja que la també potera Meri Muñoz també té contracte fins al 2027.
